# Bash4LLM‚Å∫ (v2.6.0)

> Lightweight, zero-dependency, security-hardened POSIX Bash framework and CLI client for interacting with Large Language Model (LLM) APIs (including Groq, OpenAI-compatible providers, Hugging Face, Mistral, Gemini, and local endpoints).

- **Official Repository**: https://github.com/kamaludu/bash4llm
- **Documentation & Releases**: https://github.com/kamaludu/bash4llm/releases

## Overview
Bash4LLM‚Å∫ provides a pure Bash alternative to heavy Python or Node.js LLM tooling. It requires zero external language runtimes (no Python, Node.js, or Go) and runs natively on GNU/Linux, macOS, and mobile Android (Termux) environments.

## System Requirements & Dependencies

### Environment & Shell
- **Shell**: GNU Bash 4.0 or newer (POSIX compatibility layer enabled).
- **Supported Platforms**: GNU/Linux, macOS, Android (via Termux), BSD.

### Mandatory System Binaries (23 Core Utilities)
The core script performs a pre-flight sanity check for the presence of these exact 23 binaries in `$PATH`. Missing any binary triggers an immediate exit with status code `15` (`BASH4LLM_ERR_TMP`):

`bash`, `jq`, `curl`, `mktemp`, `stat`, `base64`, `find`, `awk`, `sed`, `grep`, `xargs`, `tr`, `sort`, `head`, `wc`, `tee`, `date`, `mv`, `chmod`, `cp`, `rm`, `printf`, `comm`.

*Note: The `flock` utility is optional. If missing (e.g., in Android/Termux environments), Bash4LLM‚Å∫ automatically falls back to atomic directory locking (`mkdir`).*

### Optional Extension Dependencies
- **`openssl`**: Required for the Encrypted Security Vault (`extras/security/openssl-helper.sh`) and TLS handshake diagnostics.
- **`gzip`**: Required for session log segment compression in the Session Engine (`extras/session/session-engine.sh`).
- **`shred`** (or **`dd`**): Used for secure anti-forensic data erasure during Vault destruction.

## Key Architectural & Security Features (v2.6.0)

- **Cryptographic Module Integrity**: Pre/post sourcing SHA-256 hash verification against `extras/manifest.sha256` for external providers, hooks, and extensions.
- **PII Thread Anonymization**: Automatic SHA-256 hashing (`SAFE_THREAD_ID`) for user thread IDs, ensuring zero personal or sensitive data is written in plaintext to disk (.ndjson, .json, locks, and rate-limit logs).
- **Security Vault**: AES-256-CBC at-rest API key encryption via OpenSSL with PBKDF2 key derivation (100,000 iterations) and RAM unlock context token (`_B4L_RT_CTX`).
- **Local Rate-Limiting**: 30-second sliding-window rate limiter per thread (`BASH4LLM_RATE_LIMIT`) with bypass token support (`BASH4LLM_AUTH_TOKEN`).
- **Isolated Hook Runner**: Subshell execution for pre/post hooks with API key memory stripping and zero-eval variable parsing (`FALLBACK_PAYLOAD`).
- **Advanced Session Engine**: Segmented NDJSON chat logs (`history/sessions/`) with automatic 1MB rotation and native `gzip` compression.
- **File-System Hardening**: Enforced `umask 077`, strict `700`/`600` permissions, symlink execution blocks, atomic `mv` guarantees on the same inode, null-byte (`\x00`) input validation, and explicit ban of system `/tmp`.

## Key CLI Usage Examples

### Single Prompt & Non-Interactive Input
```bash
# Standard prompt
./bash4llm "Explain quantum computing in two sentences."

# Secure input via pipe (dual TTY/stdin handling)
printf "My secret text" | ./bash4llm "Summarize this input:"

```
### Thread Lifecycle Management
```bash
# Initialize a thread structure without network call
./bash4llm --thread project_chat --init-thread

# Send message within a thread
./bash4llm --thread project_chat "Starting refactoring."

# Rename thread title
./bash4llm --rename-thread project_chat --title "Refactoring Auth Module"

# Atomically delete thread (purges logs and indexes)
./bash4llm --delete-thread project_chat

```
### Path & Configuration Inspection
```bash
./bash4llm --print-config-dir
./bash4llm --print-provider-file
./bash4llm --print-model-file groq

```
### Diagnostics, Vault & Safety Checks
```bash
./bash4llm --check-config
./bash4llm --explain-error 17
./bash4llm --vault

```
## System File-System Layout (bash4llm.d/)
```text
bash4llm.d/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ config/                  # Provider persistence & OpenSSL encrypted Vault
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ui_state/threads/    # JSON thread metadata & index (SAFE_THREAD_ID)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ models/                  # Whitelisted model catalogs (<provider>.txt)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ history/                 # Session NDJSON logs
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ threads/             # Core fallback NDJSON logs (<safe_thread_id>.ndjson)
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ sessions/            # Advanced engine rotated & gzip compressed segments
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ var/run/locks/           # Isolated 700 lock folder (models.lock, history.lock, tmp.lock)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ tmp/rates/               # Isolated 700 sliding-window rate limit transaction tracking
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ extras/                  # Extensions & integrity manifest (extras/manifest.sha256)

```

## Exit Codes
 * 10 (BASH4LLM_ERR_NO_API_KEY): Missing, unreadable, or invalid API key.
 * 11 (BASH4LLM_ERR_BAD_MODEL): Invalid, unsupported, or excluded model name.
 * 12 (BASH4LLM_ERR_CURL_FAILED): Network failure, timeout, or curl transport error.
 * 14 (BASH4LLM_ERR_NO_PROMPT): Missing input prompt or JSON payload.
 * 15 (BASH4LLM_ERR_TMP): File-system I/O, permissions, missing system binary, symlink block, or temporary lock error.
 * 16 (BASH4LLM_ERR_API): Upstream API application or HTTP status error.
 * 17 (BASH4LLM_ERR_SEC): Security policy block, rate-limit violation, file permission error, or SHA-256 integrity check failure.
## Core Documentation References
 * Repository Home: Main source code and README.
 * Technical Specification: Full architectural specification for runtime sections.
 * Changelog: Historical record of security patches, fixes, and CLI flags.
